# UC-22.4.16 · CCPA/CPRA — Automated Decision Profiling — Human Review Queue Depth for Adverse Automated Eligibility Decisions

## Description

When automated decisions adversely affect consumers, human review pathways must function. Monitoring queue age for appeals ensures operational readiness beyond policy language—supporting transparency and remedy expectations.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for CCPA/CPRA compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes itsm and sourcetypes snow:sc_req_item. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Map `cat_item` to your catalog entry; (2) tune SLA hours per CPRA operational policy; (3) executive dashboard for legal; (4) integrate with chat alerts; (5) monthly remediation report. Indexes required: `index=itsm`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=snow:sc_req_item`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=itsm` `sourcetype="snow:sc_req_item"` (number, cat_item, opened_at, state, assignment_group).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Map `cat_item` to your catalog entry; (2) tune SLA hours per CPRA operational policy; (3) executive dashboard for legal; (4) integrate with chat alerts; (5) monthly remediation report.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:sc_req_item" cat_item="privacy_automated_decision_review" state!="Closed" earliest=-180d
| eval opened=strptime(opened_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval age_h=round((now()-opened)/3600,1)
| where age_h>72
| stats avg(age_h) as avg_age_h, max(age_h) as max_age_h, count by assignment_group
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**CCPA/CPRA — Automated Decision Profiling — Human Review Queue Depth for Adverse Automated Eligibility Decisions** — When automated decisions adversely affect consumers, human review pathways must function. Monitoring queue age for appeals ensures operational readiness beyond policy language—supporting transparency and remedy expectations.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=itsm` `sourcetype="snow:sc_req_item"` (number, cat_item, opened_at, state, assignment_group). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: itsm; **sourcetype**: snow:sc_req_item. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=itsm, sourcetype="snow:sc_req_item", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **opened** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **age_h** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where age_h>72` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by assignment_group** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare ticket and field counts with the ServiceNow ITSM or GRC console (same time range) and confirm synchronization fields and sourcetypes used by this search.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (queues), Single value (oldest age_h), Column chart by group.

## SPL

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:sc_req_item" cat_item="privacy_automated_decision_review" state!="Closed" earliest=-180d
| eval opened=strptime(opened_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval age_h=round((now()-opened)/3600,1)
| where age_h>72
| stats avg(age_h) as avg_age_h, max(age_h) as max_age_h, count by assignment_group
| sort - count
```

## Visualization

Table (queues), Single value (oldest age_h), Column chart by group.
